interpretationBoundary: Supplementary semantic-query coverage only; not pooled into the realworld-active thesis denominators.

scenarios.displayName: Fill sign-in email by accessible label

excludedApps.reason: The Angular sign-in email field is placeholder-only and has no genuine label contract.
The Svelte sign-in email field is placeholder-only and has no genuine label contract.

scenarios.targetInteractionOrAssertion: Vue exposes the sign-in email control with a stable aria-label, making getByLabel the natural user-facing contract.
scenarios.semanticNaturalnessRationale: Accessible labels are the user-facing contract for labeled form controls; Vue provides this via aria-label.
scenarios.supplementaryOnlyRationale: The main corpus naturally exercises sign-in through placeholder/role locators, so this scenario isolates label-based semantic behavior.

scenarios.targetInteractionOrAssertion: The comment textarea is consistently exposed with the visible placeholder "Write a comment..." across the three apps.
scenarios.semanticNaturalnessRationale: The textarea lacks a stronger shared label contract, while the placeholder is stable, visible, and usable as a fallback contract.
scenarios.supplementaryOnlyRationale: The main corpus includes comment creation, but this scenario explicitly reports placeholder-query coverage as supplementary evidence.

scenarios.displayName: Assert article title by visible text
scenarios.targetInteractionOrAssertion: An API-provisioned article title is visible content whose text is the intended user-facing assertion contract.
scenarios.semanticNaturalnessRationale: The scenario asserts content whose visible string is the contract; getByText expresses that directly.
scenarios.supplementaryOnlyRationale: The primary title assertion uses the family-standard title locator; this supplementary case isolates text-query evidence.

scenarios.displayName: Assert profile avatar by alternative text

excludedApps.reason: The Angular profile avatar image has no stable alt text, so getByAltText would be artificial.

scenarios.targetInteractionOrAssertion: Svelte and Vue expose profile avatars with alt text equal to the username, making getByAltText a natural image locator.
scenarios.semanticNaturalnessRationale: Alternative text is the user-facing contract for meaningful images and is present on the profile avatar in Svelte and Vue.
scenarios.supplementaryOnlyRationale: The active corpus does not benchmark image alternative-text queries, so this scenario adds bounded supplementary coverage.

exclusionPolicy: Unsupported app/scenario pairs are reported and not executed; intended semantic strategies do not silently fall back to getByRole.
denominatorPolicy: Supplementary semantic results are stored and aggregated separately from realworld-active and are not pooled into primary thesis denominators.
